title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: lingamr provides five sample data generators. returns list containing data (data frame) true_adjacency (true adjacency matrix).

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: generate_lingam_sample_6() returns artificial data following 6-variable LiNGAM model, together true adjacency matrix. data stored data adjacency matrix true_adjacency. plot_adjacency() draws causal graph based adjacency matrix.

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: lingam_direct() runs Direct LiNGAM. default, independence assessed using mutual information, path coefficients computed adaptive LASSO regression. use HSIC assessing independence, set measure argument “kernel”. Note HSIC computationally expensive.

what: Comparing the Estimated and True Structures
title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: true structure known, sample data, can pass true adjacency matrix true_B argument plot_adjacency() color-code estimated edges comparing true structure. lets assess estimation accuracy glance, useful validating methods educational purposes. Green (solid): correctly detected edges (estimated true) Red (solid): falsely detected edges (estimated true) Orange (dashed): missed edges (true estimated; true coefficient shown)

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: Multiple regression coefficients total causal effects agree mediating variables present. true causal structure generate_lingam_sample_6(), two paths x3 x1 (direct edge x3 x1). x3 -> x0 -> x1 (indirect effect: 3.0 x 3.0 = 9.0) x3 -> x2 -> x1 (indirect effect: 6.0 x 2.0 = 12.0) Total causal effect x3 x1 = 9.0 + 12.0 = 21.0 compare coefficients OLS regression includes variables predict x1 results estimate_all_total_effects(). OLS coefficient x3 nearly 0. including x0 x2 (mediating variables) model causes x3’s “effect mediation” absorbed coefficients x0 x2. contrast, value x3 estimate_all_total_effects() ~21, correctly represents much x1 ultimately changes x3 moved one unit. want know “ultimate impact intervening variable,” use total causal effect rather multiple regression coefficient.
code: # Multiple regression: include all variables to predict x1 lm_coefs <- coef(lm(x1 ~ ., data = x1k$data))  # Comparison (variables causally related to x1: x0, x2, x3) data.frame(   variable           = c("x0", "x2", "x3"),   OLS_coefficient    = round(lm_coefs[c("x0", "x2", "x3")], 3),   total_causal_effect = round(total_effects["x1", c("x0", "x2", "x3")], 3) ) #>    variable OLS_coefficient total_causal_effect #> x0       x0           3.237               2.897 #> x2       x2           1.965               1.910 #> x3       x3           0.014              21.059

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: theoretical heart LiNGAM assumption error terms follow non-Gaussian distribution. errors Gaussian, direction causation becomes fundamentally unidentifiable (reverse-direction model explains distribution exists), estimates unreliable. verify difference practice switching error distribution noise_dist argument generate_lingam_sample_6(). true structure follows (root x3).

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: test normality residuals. LiNGAM assumes non-Gaussianity, normality rejected (small p-value) consistent model’s assumptions. also check normality residuals QQ plot.
code: # Shapiro-Wilk (default) x1k$data |>   test_residual_normality(result) #> === Residual Normality Test === #> Method:         shapiro #> Sample size:    1000 #> Significance:   0.050 #> Non-Gaussian:   6 / 6 variables #>  #>  variable statistic   p_value is_non_gauss skewness kurtosis #>        x0    0.9516 < 2.2e-16         TRUE    0.061   -1.215 #>        x1    0.9521 < 2.2e-16         TRUE    0.026   -1.213 #>        x2    0.9557 < 2.2e-16         TRUE    0.083   -1.170 #>        x3    0.9578  2.25e-16         TRUE    0.025   -1.163 #>        x4    0.9546 < 2.2e-16         TRUE   -0.003   -1.205 #>        x5    0.9536 < 2.2e-16         TRUE   -0.052   -1.206 #>  #> Interpretation: #>   is_non_gauss = TRUE  -> rejects normality (supports LiNGAM assumption) #>   is_non_gauss = FALSE -> cannot reject normality (LiNGAM may not fit) #>  #> All residuals are non-Gaussian. LiNGAM assumption is supported. x1k$data |>   plot_residual_qq(result)

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: summary_lingam() runs residual independence test normality test together, letting review glance well two assumptions LiNGAM relies hold (residuals mutually independent, residuals non-Gaussian). Instead calling get_error_independence_p_values() test_residual_normality() separately, can survey diagnostics one place.
code: x1k$data |>   summary_lingam(result) #> === Direct LiNGAM Model Summary === #> Variables:    6 #> Observations: 1000 #> Edges:        7 #> Causal order: x3 -> x2 -> x0 -> x4 -> x5 -> x1 #>  #> --- Assumption 1: Independence of residuals --- #> Method:           spearman #> Dependent pairs:  0 / 15  (p < 0.050) #> Min p-value:      0.1002 #> => Residuals appear mutually independent (assumption supported). #>  #> --- Assumption 2: Non-Gaussianity of residuals --- #> Method:           shapiro #> Non-Gaussian:     6 / 6  (p <= 0.050) #> => All residuals are non-Gaussian (assumption supported).

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: assess reliability model using bootstrap method. number iterations variables large, specifying parallel = TRUE lets run faster multiple cores. number cores specified n_cores (unspecified, capped 2 cores safety). Note parallel execution uses L’Ecuyer’s parallel random number streams, results reproducible given seed n_cores, numerically match results sequential execution (parallel = FALSE).
code: bs_model <- x1k$data |>   lingam_direct_bootstrap(n_sampling = 100L, seed = 42) #> Bootstrap: 100 iterations, method=adaptive_lasso (sequential) #>   iteration 1 / 100 #>   iteration 10 / 100 #>   iteration 20 / 100 #>   iteration 30 / 100 #>   iteration 40 / 100 #>   iteration 50 / 100 #>   iteration 60 / 100 #>   iteration 70 / 100 #>   iteration 80 / 100 #>   iteration 90 / 100 #>   iteration 100 / 100 #> Completed in 3.2 seconds.  bs_model #> BootstrapResult: 100 samplings, 6 features bs_model <- x1k$data |>   lingam_direct_bootstrap(     n_sampling = 100L,     seed       = 42,     parallel   = TRUE,     n_cores    = 4L   )

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: analyze 6-variable dataset (n=1000n = 1000) methods.
code: d_cmp <- generate_lingam_sample_6(n = 1000, seed = 42)  t_cmp_direct <- system.time(res_cmp_direct <- lingam_direct(d_cmp$data)) t_cmp_ica    <- system.time(res_cmp_ica    <- pcalg::lingam(as.matrix(d_cmp$data)))  cat(sprintf("Direct LiNGAM : %.2f sec\nICA-LiNGAM    : %.2f sec\n",             t_cmp_direct["elapsed"], t_cmp_ica["elapsed"])) #> Direct LiNGAM : 0.01 sec #> ICA-LiNGAM    : 0.02 sec

title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: $Bpruned uses convention lingamr adjacency matrix (B[, j] = coefficient xj→xix_j \x_i).
code: B_ica <- res_cmp_ica$Bpruned rownames(B_ica) <- colnames(B_ica) <- names(d_cmp$data)  idx_ica  <- which(abs(B_ica) > 0, arr.ind = TRUE) tidy_ica <- data.frame(   from  = colnames(B_ica)[idx_ica[, 2]],   to    = rownames(B_ica)[idx_ica[, 1]],   ica   = round(B_ica[idx_ica], 3) )  tidy_dir <- tidy(res_cmp_direct) tidy_dir <- data.frame(from = tidy_dir$from, to = tidy_dir$to,                        direct = round(tidy_dir$estimate, 3))  merge(tidy_dir, tidy_ica, by = c("from", "to"), sort = TRUE) #>   from to direct    ica #> 1   x0 x1  2.988  3.245 #> 2   x0 x4  8.017  7.999 #> 3   x0 x5  4.015  3.876 #> 4   x2 x1  2.002  1.973 #> 5   x2 x4 -1.009 -1.060 #> 6   x3 x0  3.033  3.027 #> 7   x3 x2  5.993  6.101

text: compare structures full outer join edges check consistency true DAG. direct ica column NA, means method detect edge. truth = TRUE indicates edge exists true DAG.
code: B_true   <- d_cmp$true_adjacency idx_true <- which(abs(B_true) > 0, arr.ind = TRUE) true_key <- paste(colnames(B_true)[idx_true[, 2]],                   rownames(B_true)[idx_true[, 1]], sep = "->")  cmp <- merge(tidy_dir, tidy_ica, by = c("from", "to"), all = TRUE, sort = TRUE) cmp$truth <- paste(cmp$from, cmp$to, sep = "->") %in% true_key cmp #>   from to direct    ica truth #> 1   x0 x1  2.988  3.245  TRUE #> 2   x0 x4  8.017  7.999  TRUE #> 3   x0 x5  4.015  3.876  TRUE #> 4   x2 x1  2.002  1.973  TRUE #> 5   x2 x4 -1.009 -1.060  TRUE #> 6   x3 x0  3.033  3.027  TRUE #> 7   x3 x2  5.993  6.101  TRUE

what: When There Are Many Variables: The Scalability Wall
title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: step, Direct LiNGAM performs independence tests remaining pairs variables. Since number steps pp number tests per step p(p−1)p(p-1), total number independence tests approximately ∑k=1pk(k−1)≈p33\sum_{k=1}^{p} k(k-1) \approx \frac{p^3}{3} giving computational cost O(p3)O(p^3). contrast, FastICA used ICA-LiNGAM O(p2n)O(p^2 n) (BLAS optimization), gap widens number variables grows. generate_lingam_large_sample() generates random sparse DAG data freely configurable number variables p. variable xix_i (≥1i \ge 1) randomly max_parents parents chosen x0,…,xi−1x_0, \ldots, x_{-1}. Since causal order guaranteed follow index order, adjacency matrix always lower triangular matrix.

what: A Case Where DirectLiNGAM Struggles: The Measurement Error Paradox
title: Causal Discovery with lingamr
text: Causal discovery methods assumptions, violated may fail recover correct structure. generate_lingam_paradox_data() dataset designed deliberately create difficult case. sample generators, returns list containing data true_adjacency. true structure data simple serial chain x0 -> x1 -> x2 -> x3 (coefficient 0.8). However, two notable features. Heavy measurement error added root variable x0. disrupts independence assessment performed DirectLiNGAM’s first step, causing choose root incorrectly making error propagation likely. variables standardized scale() (differences scale). visualize true causal graph. coefficient 0.8 structural coefficient latent scale standardization. Now let us apply Direct LiNGAM. Note head estimated causal order x1, true root x0. measurement error root, DirectLiNGAM fails select x0 first exogenous variable. downstream x1 -> x2 -> x3 recovered correctly, direction x0 x1 reversed (truth x0 -> x1, estimate x1 -> x0), x0 ends treated almost like sink. use bootstrap check whether error occurred chance systematic. important point incorrect direction x1 -> x0 reproduced nearly 100% probability. words, error coincidental systematic, appears stably across bootstrap samples. Lesson: Bootstrap stability (high reproduction probability) guarantee correctness estimate. model’s assumptions (, assumption “upstream variables measurement error”) violated, method may stably recover incorrect structure. important evaluate results critically, together tests residual independence normality domain knowledge data-generating process.

what: Why Direct LiNGAM slows down with large p
title: Generate large-scale sample data to benchmark Direct LiNGAM scalability — generate_lingam_large_sample
text: p steps, Direct LiNGAM evaluates independence measure every remaining candidate root every residual. total number evaluations : $$\sum_{k=1}^{p} k(k-1) \approx \frac{p^3}{3}$$ .e., O(p^3). evaluation O(n), giving O(p^3 n) overall. p = 10 330 evaluations; p = 20 2,660; p = 40 21,320 — 8x increase every time p doubles.

title: Generate large-scale sample data to benchmark Direct LiNGAM scalability — generate_lingam_large_sample
text: ICA-LiNGAM applies FastICA whole p x n data matrix. FastICA iteration costs O(p^2 n), algorithm typically converges far fewer p iterations.  Additionally, matrix operations fully vectorised (BLAS/LAPACK), whereas Direct LiNGAM iterates pairs R loop.

title: Generate large-scale sample data to benchmark Direct LiNGAM scalability — generate_lingam_large_sample
text: Variables topologically ordered x0, x1, ..., x(p-1). >= 1, number parents sampled uniformly 1 min(max_parents, ), parents drawn without replacement x0, ..., x(-1).  Edge coefficients drawn uniformly [-coef_max, -coef_min] U [coef_min, coef_max]. resulting adjacency matrix strictly lower-triangular.

title: Direct LiNGAM — lingam_direct
text: X Numeric matrix (n_samples x n_features), data frame matrix prior_knowledge Prior knowledge matrix (n_features x n_features) NULL. 0: directed path x_i x_j 1: directed path x_i x_j -1: unknown apply_prior_knowledge_softly Whether apply prior knowledge softly (logical) measure Independence evaluation measure ("pwling" "kernel") reg_method Regression method adjacency matrix estimation. "ols": ordinary least squares, "lasso": LASSO regression, "adaptive_lasso": adaptive LASSO regression (default), "ridge": Ridge regression (robust multicollinearity; perform sparse estimation). lambda LASSO penalty (lambda) selection. "lambda.min" : minimum CV prediction error, prioritizes prediction accuracy. "lambda.1se" : CV 1SE rule, robust less prone overfitting. "AIC": minimum AIC. Fast. "BIC": minimum BIC. Fast, sparsest. Default. "oracle" : adaptive LASSO regression . Selects lambda guarantees oracle property. Fast. init_method Method estimating initial weights adaptive LASSO regression. "ols": ordinary least squares (default), "ridge": Ridge regression. Ridge regression recommended multicollinearity suspected.

text: list class lingam_summary, containing following elements: n_variables, n_samples: Number variables / number observations causal_order: Causal order (variable-name labels) n_edges: Number nonzero elements adjacency matrix (number estimated edges) independence_p_values: Matrix p-values independence test residuals n_dependent_pairs, n_pairs: Number pairs p < alpha / total number pairs min_independence_p: Minimum p-value independence test normality: Result normality test (lingam_normality_test object) n_non_gaussian: Number variables judged non-Gaussian alpha, independence_method, normality_method: settings used

title: Summarize the goodness-of-fit of a Direct LiNGAM model at once — summary_lingam
text: Gaussian-likelihood-based criteria BIC/AIC included theoretically inconsistent LiNGAM's assumption "errors non-Gaussian". Instead, verification results assumptions summarized.

title: Summarize the goodness-of-fit of a Direct LiNGAM model at once — summary_lingam
code: LiNGAM_sample_1000 <- generate_lingam_sample_6()  model <- lingam_direct(LiNGAM_sample_1000$data, reg_method = "ols")  summary_lingam(LiNGAM_sample_1000$data, model) #> === Direct LiNGAM Model Summary === #> Variables:    6 #> Observations: 1000 #> Edges:        15 #> Causal order: x3 -> x2 -> x0 -> x4 -> x5 -> x1 #>  #> --- Assumption 1: Independence of residuals --- #> Method:           spearman #> Dependent pairs:  0 / 15  (p < 0.050) #> Min p-value:      0.9187 #> => Residuals appear mutually independent (assumption supported). #>  #> --- Assumption 2: Non-Gaussianity of residuals --- #> Method:           shapiro #> Non-Gaussian:     6 / 6  (p <= 0.050) #> => All residuals are non-Gaussian (assumption supported).

title: Test normality of residuals from Direct LiNGAM — test_residual_normality
code: # Load the sample data LiNGAM_sample_1000 <- generate_lingam_sample_6()  # Run Direct LiNGAM result <- lingam_direct(LiNGAM_sample_1000$data)  # Shapiro-Wilk (default) test_residual_normality(LiNGAM_sample_1000$data, result) #> === Residual Normality Test === #> Method:         shapiro #> Sample size:    1000 #> Significance:   0.050 #> Non-Gaussian:   6 / 6 variables #>  #>  variable statistic   p_value is_non_gauss skewness kurtosis #>        x0    0.9516 < 2.2e-16         TRUE    0.061   -1.215 #>        x1    0.9521 < 2.2e-16         TRUE    0.026   -1.213 #>        x2    0.9557 < 2.2e-16         TRUE    0.083   -1.170 #>        x3    0.9578  2.25e-16         TRUE    0.025   -1.163 #>        x4    0.9546 < 2.2e-16         TRUE   -0.003   -1.205 #>        x5    0.9536 < 2.2e-16         TRUE   -0.052   -1.206 #>  #> Interpretation: #>   is_non_gauss = TRUE  -> rejects normality (supports LiNGAM assumption) #>   is_non_gauss = FALSE -> cannot reject normality (LiNGAM may not fit) #>  #> All residuals are non-Gaussian. LiNGAM assumption is supported.

title: lingamr 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission. Direct LiNGAM (lingam_direct()) selectable regression backends adjacency-matrix estimation via reg_method: ordinary least squares ("ols"), LASSO ("lasso"), adaptive LASSO ("adaptive_lasso"), ridge regression ("ridge"). lingam_direct_bootstrap() provides bootstrap stability assessment, including causal-order stability, supports multi-core execution parallel n_cores arguments (via parallel::makePSOCKcluster()). Sequential execution remains default. Parallel runs use L’Ecuyer parallel RNG streams, results reproducible given seed/n_cores differ numerically sequential path. Model diagnostics: residual independence normality tests, plus one-call summary_lingam(). Visualization DiagrammeR (interactive) ggplot2 autoplot() (static). broom-style tidiers (tidy() / glance()).
